item.title: ‘Savvy the Squirrel’ has missed a trick
item.description: If there’s one place cash does not belong, it’s in a Junior Cash Isa — so why doesn’t the government promote investing for your children?

item.title: How can I speak ill of the dead?
item.description: Three tricks for avoiding cancellation

item.title: ‘Big two’ parties risk extinction
item.description: Broxbourne and Hammersmith were small bright spots for Tories and Labour, but they cannot diminish the terrible performance

item.title: Quarterly reporting: when is less important than what
item.description: Plus, Norway’s rate increase. And dishwashers.

item.title: Humans still matter more than AI in finance
item.description: Recruiting digital natives with critical thinking skills is going to be crucial

item.title: Protectionism makes everyone poorer during global shocks
item.description: When countries act to insulate domestic markets, the result is often higher prices and greater volatility

item.title: Trump’s generational boost to US savings should be expanded
item.description: Congress should introduce auto-enrolment for American Dream Accounts and widen the eligibility for savings incentives

item.title: Tulip mania at Longwood Gardens is filled with tips to take home
item.description: Despite wild temperature swings, the Pennsylvania heartland of horticulture puts on a riotous spring display, with many a trick worth imitating

item.title: Tech boom brings emerging markets and their rich cousins closer together
item.description: Asian manufacturers supplying the picks and shovels of the AI bonanza could be on a more durable footing

item.title: Trump’s tariffs and troop cuts could hurt America more than Europe
item.description: As the White House lashes out against Germany, US allies must get serious about strategic autonomy

item.title: Old IT makes its bid for AI relevance
item.description: Pendulum is swinging back to companies in areas such as servers, more general chips and software

item.title: Why software firms are calling time on the SaaSpocalypse
item.description: SaaS companies can stay relevant by housing customer data and layering AI on top

item.title: David Attenborough: nature’s great communicator
item.description: At 100, his work should inspire a new generation of scientific truth-tellers

item.title: Polymarket anonymity must end
item.description: Without identity checks, prediction market regulation will be toothless

item.title: Let’s stop blaming retail investors for wonky markets
item.description: They are finance’s saviours as well as its villains

item.title: Who will drive the driverless car revolution?
item.description: US companies still lead in innovation but their Chinese rivals are speeding ahead in the deployment race

item.title: Why I don’t write about the Liberal Democrats
item.description: Britain’s third party is being drowned out by noisier insurgents

item.title: Can I protect my pension from my ex?
item.description: We don’t have children and I don’t want our shared house

item.title: How the oilman’s president boosted a green transition
item.description: Donald Trump’s Iran war has made fossil fuels expensive and unreliable

item.title: The affordability problem consuming Trump’s presidency
item.description: Previous US presidents have been undone by oil price shocks, but rarely ones of their own making

item.title: Trading the trends in GDP
item.description: Plus, yen intervention

item.title: Europe’s banks hope ‘friendshoring’ will apply to them too
item.description: Geopolitical tensions have raised concerns about US services becoming harder to obtain if relationships sour

item.title: Apple, Berkshire and the virtue of patience
item.description: Waiting for outstanding opportunities has paid off but it is getting increasingly difficult

item.title: Russia’s return to Venice risks rendering the Biennale irrelevant
item.description: At the art world’s most important event, culture is again being used in the service of state power

item.title: Why the shift from savings glut to grab is good for investors
item.description: Countries and companies will need to compete for funds to meet their spending plans
